title: Traffic Diverted by AI! Traditional Media and Tech Giants Fall Out, Reddit May Stop Feeding Google
excerpt: With the popularity of artificial intelligence search, traditional web media are facing their greatest traffic crisis in history. More and more users have devel
content_html:
With the popularity of artificial intelligence search, traditional web media are facing their greatest traffic crisis in history. More and more users have developed the habit of getting answers from AI, leading to a significant decrease in traffic to media websites themselves.

The Traffic Crisis Forces the Termination of Collaborations

Reddit, a well-known community platform, previously had a contract with Google worth $60 million per year, allowing its content to be used for training AI models. However, as AI search gradually replaces the need for users to search forums, Reddit is reevaluating whether to continue this collaboration.

A similar situation is also frequently occurring at traditional news organizations. Prominent media outlets such as Reuters and USA Today are considering completely blocking Google's crawlers from accessing their website content.

The Media Industry Begins a Content Defense War

To counter the erosion of traffic caused by AI, the media platform Politico is considering automated prevention measures such as registration walls. Meanwhile, The Economist is actively discussing a withdrawal from the AI search field and taking measures to protect its intellectual property rights.

Facing this difficulty, some media outlets are trying alternative survival strategies. Time magazine has tried hiding ads that are not visible to regular readers, aiming to allow advertisers to reach potential customers when AI crawlers access the content.

sentences.zh: 随着人工智能搜索的普及，传统网络媒体面临着有史以来最大的流量危机。

content_html_zh:
随着人工智能搜索的普及，传统网络媒体面临着有史以来最大的流量危机。 越来越多的用户养成了从人工智能那里获取答案的习惯，导致媒体网站本身的流量大幅下降。

交通危机迫使合作终止

知名社区平台Reddit此前与谷歌签订了每年价值6000万美元的合同，允许其内容用于训练AI模型。 然而，随着AI搜索逐渐取代用户搜索论坛的需要，Reddit正在重新评估是否继续这种合作。

类似的情况在传统新闻机构也屡屡发生。 路透社和《今日美国》等知名媒体正在考虑完全阻止谷歌的爬虫访问其网站内容。

媒体行业开始内容防御战

为了应对人工智能造成的流量侵蚀，媒体平台 Politico 正在考虑登记墙等自动化预防措施。 与此同时，《经济学人》正在积极讨论退出人工智能搜索领域，并采取措施保护其知识产权。

面对这一困难，一些媒体正在尝试替代的生存策略。 《时代》杂志曾尝试隐藏普通读者看不到的广告，旨在让广告商在人工智能爬虫访问内容时接触到潜在客户。
